preview_text:
The model rates Croatia as the likely winner at 47%, though the margin over Panama is moderate rather than decisive. Panama's win probability stands at 23%, leaving a gap of roughly 23 percentage points in Croatia's favor. The underdog enters with a genuine path to victory, not merely a technical possibility.

The 30% draw probability is the second-most likely outcome in the model's forecast, suggesting this match has competitive texture. The spread across outcomes—no single scenario commanding more than half the probability mass—indicates a relatively open contest. A draw is plausible enough to shape team approach; neither side faces overwhelming pressure toward a decisive result.

Model and market diverge meaningfully on the favorite. The market consensus, drawn from 44 sportsbooks and corroborated by Polymarket, places Croatia at 61%, roughly 14 percentage points higher than the model's 47%. The gap widens in Croatia's favor across all three outcomes. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better captures match reality; the divergence flags a material disagreement worth noting.
